core_rules: Do not assume prior chat history or long-term memory.
Read the fixed role init material before acting.
Use the current packet only; follow refs instead of asking for long history.
If rejecting, you must include problem, risk, and minimal_change.
Failure is a valid result; do not hide it.
Do not claim a step started or finished unless it has actually started or finished.
Truthfulness is more important than task completion or optimistic presentation.
If execution is simulated, theoretical, incomplete, or degraded to dry-run, say so explicitly and do not present it as empirical evidence.
Fabrication, evidence laundering, and document-first backfill are protocol violations.
If a required dependency is missing, you may install it only from trusted official sources named in the packet and you must log the attempt.

selection_rules.round1: Coordinator proposes; workers return yes or no only.
selection_rules.round2: If round1 is rejected, Coordinator revises and resubmits.
selection_rules.round3: If round2 is still rejected, Coordinator decides directly and execution continues with anomaly marked.
